title: Rojasia bornmuelleri
intro: Rojasia bornmuelleri, also known as the Himalayan Rojasia, is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae and is known to be a hemiparasitic perennial herb. The plant has been found to have potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Rojasia bornmuelleri include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Rojasia bornmuelleri is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Rojasia bornmuelleri is classified within the family Oenotheraceae, commonly known as the evening primrose family. This classification places Rojasia bornmuelleri alongside other flowering plants that are native to the mountainous regions of China and surrounding areas. The genus Rojasia is typically found in the Himalayas and adjacent parts of Asia. Rojasia bornmuelleri specifically belongs to the order Myrtales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification may include the subclass Dilleniidae and the superorder Rosanae.
